seed_problem: Select a maximum-weight set of mutually non-conflicting research sites on a general conflict graph; checker validates independence and scores weight/baseline.
mutation: input: skin the max-weight-independent-set selection as satellite ground-station activation and place the conflict relation on a general (non-bipartite, possibly dense) interference graph with per-vertex scientific values, so the easy bipartite/interval special cases vanish and constructing the optimum conflict-free set is NP-hard.
title: Satellite Ground-Station Activation
strategies: trivial baseline: activate only the single highest-value station (matches checker baseline, ratio ~0.1)
weight-descending greedy: consider stations from highest value to lowest, activating each one whose activation introduces no conflict with the already-activated set
degree-aware greedy: order stations by value/(conflict-degree+1) so high-value low-interference stations are taken first, yielding larger conflict-free sets on dense graphs
multi-restart randomized greedy + maximal extension: run many seeded value-weighted random orderings, greedily build a maximal conflict-free set from each, and keep the highest-value set found
